title: Who won the 2025-26 La Liga (Spanish top-flight football)?
rules: If Real Madrid wins the La Liga, then the market resolves to Yes.
tavily_brief: [redacted] According to Goal.com standings data, Barcelona sits at 91 points from 36 matches (30W-1D-5L), while Real Madrid has 80 points from 36 matches (24W-5D-6L). This represents a dramatic shift from earlier in the season, when the title race remained highly competitive with 16 lead changes through February. Barcelona's recent form has been exceptional. The Opta supercomputer (as of late February) gave Barcelona a 60.2% title probability versus Real Madrid's 39.6%, and Barcelona has since extended their advantage significantly. Real Madrid suffered consecutive defeats to Osasuna and Getafe in late April, derailing their title momentum. Barcelona, by contrast, won nine consecutive league matches over a two-month stretch, demonstrating superior consistency. The Opta analysis noted Barcelona's ability to win 18 points from losing positions across the season, compared to just five points for Madrid in similar situations. The remaining fixture list heavily favors Barcelona's position. With five matches left (Osasuna away, Espanyol away, Real Madrid home, Alavés away, Real Betis home), Barcelona can mathematically clinch the title as early as May 3 if they [redacted] The critical El Clásico on May 10 at Camp Nou could serve as the title-clinching match. Real Madrid would need to win all remaining matches while Barcelona collapse dramatically—an outcome multiple sources describe as requiring an "unthinkable" Barcelona collapse. Historically, either Barcelona or Real Madrid has won 18 of the last 20 La Liga titles, with no other team winning since Atlético Madrid in 2020-21. Barcelona won the 2024-25 title by four points and are seeking back-to-back championships for the first time since 2018-19. **Most important facts:** (1) Barcelona's 11-point lead with five matches remaining makes their title probability extremely high; (2) Real Madrid's recent poor form (5 wins in last 8 matches) contrasts sharply with Barcelona's dominance; (3) The May 10 El Clásico at Camp Nou could clinch the title for Barcelona.

args.reasoning:
Evidence inventory:
- Real Madrid beat Valencia 4-0 on Nov 1, 2025 (record 10-0-1 = 10 wins, 0 draws, 1 loss = 30 points after 11 matches).
- Real Madrid beat Barcelona 2-1 in El Clasico on Oct 26, 2025.
- Real Madrid's only loss was 5-2 to Atletico Madrid on Sept 27.
- Barcelona lost to Real Sociedad in late September and to Sevilla 4-1 on Oct 5, plus the Clasico loss — they have multiple league losses.

Strong implication: Real Madrid is clearly leading La Liga in early November 2025 with ~30 points after 11 games, while Barcelona has dropped significant points. The Kalshi prices listed (Barcelona @ 0.99, Real Madrid @ 0.02) appear to be INVERTED relative to the actual title race — this looks like either a data error or the prices reflect last season's champion (Barcelona retained crown in 2024-25). 

Given current standings clearly favor Real Madrid with a lead over Barcelona at matchday 11, with ~27 matches remaining the race is far from decided but Real Madrid is the clear favorite. Historical base rates: leaders after 11 matches with this kind of form win roughly 55-65% of the time. Barcelona remains a serious threat (defending champs, strong squad) maybe 30-35%. Atletico Madrid beat Real Madrid 5-2 and is a dark horse ~5-8%. Other clubs negligible.

I will ignore the Kalshi prices as they appear inverted/erroneous and forecast based on the on-pitch evidence: Real Madrid leading, beat Barcelona head-to-head, strong form.

agent_results.reasoning: Kalshi snapshot has Barcelona at 0.99 indicating they have effectively clinched or are mathematically nearly certain to win. Evidence describes Barcelona with massive lead and Real Madrid in poor form. Other teams have negligible chances given the two-team duopoly in La Liga.

Evidence shows Barcelona with 11-point lead over Real Madrid with 5 matches left in 2024-25 season (note: evidence appears to be from prior season, but Kalshi snapshot suggests Barcelona dominant). Market has Barcelona at 0.99, Real Madrid at 0.02. No strong signal contradicts market. Stay near market.

P1: Kalshi snapshot shows Barcelona @ 0.99, Real Madrid @ 0.02, indicating the title race is effectively decided in Barcelona's favor. Evidence (though referencing late-season standings) shows Barcelona with a commanding lead and Real Madrid suffering consecutive defeats to Osasuna and Getafe, with only 5 wins in last 8. P2: Base rate — Real Madrid/Barcelona win ~90% of La Liga titles historically

Evidence describes Barcelona with 91 points after 36 matches, 11-point lead over Real Madrid with 5 matches remaining—essentially title clinched. While some text references the 2024-25 season, the Kalshi T-3d snapshot strongly aligns: Barcelona @ 0.99, Real Madrid @ 0.02, all others ≤0.01. The market is effectively resolved with Barcelona as champions. No specific evidence contradicts Barcelona's

Barcelona lead La Liga with 91 points from 36 matches (30W-1D-5L), 11 points clear of Real Madrid on 80 from 36, with five fixtures remaining; they’ve won nine straight league games and can clinch as early as the May 10 El Clásico at Camp Nou. Real Madrid’s title push was hit by consecutive late-April defeats to Osasuna and Getafe, while Barcelona have repeatedly rescued results (18 points from lo

Evidence inventory:
- Real Madrid beat Valencia 4-0 on Nov 1, 2025 (record 10-0-1 = 10 wins, 0 draws, 1 loss = 30 points after 11 matches).
- Real Madrid beat Barcelona 2-1 in El Clasico on Oct 26, 2025.
- Real Madrid's only loss was 5-2 to Atletico Madrid on Sept 27.
- Barcelona lost to Real Sociedad in late September and to Sevilla 4-1 on Oct 5, plus the Clasico loss — they have multiple league losses.

Strong implication: Real Madrid is clearly leading La Liga in early November 2025 with ~30 points after 11 games, while Barcelona has dropped significant points. The Kalshi prices listed (Barcelona @ 0.99, Real Madrid @ 0.02) appear to be INVERTED relative to the actual title race — this looks like either a data error or the prices reflect last season's champion (Barcelona retained crown in 2024-25). 

Given current standings clearly favor Real Madrid with a lead over Barcelona at matchday 11, with ~27 matches remaining the race is far from decided but Real Madrid is the clear favorite. Historical base rates: leaders after 11 matches with this kind of form win roughly 55-65% of the time. Barcelona remains a serious threat (defending champs, strong squad) maybe 30-35%. Atletico Madrid beat Real Madrid 5-2 and is a dark horse ~5-8%. Other clubs negligible.

I will ignore the Kalshi prices as they appear inverted/erroneous and forecast based on the on-pitch evidence: Real Madrid leading, beat Barcelona head-to-head, strong form.

iterations.content:
The Kalshi snapshot is the critical signal here. The question asks who won the 2025-26 La Liga (i.e., the season that started August 2025). The Kalshi prices show Barcelona at 0.990 and Real Madrid at 0.020, all others at 0.005-0.010. This is a near-certainty pre-resolution market.

However, there's a major discrepancy: the evidence summary appears to be from the 2024-25 season (Barcelona did win La Liga 2024-25). For 2025-26, we're only in November 2025 with the season ongoing. DEEP_0's evidence (Real Madrid beat Barcelona 2-1 Oct 26 2025, Atletico beat Real Madrid 5-2 Sept 27 2025) is actually consistent with my search results from this season.

Wait — but the Kalshi price is at 0.99 for Barcelona. That would only make sense if this is end-of-season. Let me reconsider — the question may have already resolved, or the Kalshi snapshot is "T-3d" (3 days before resolution), meaning the question is about to resolve and Barcelona is heavily favored.

Actually, looking again: Kalshi prices are explicit and at "T-3d snapshot" — meaning resolution is imminent. At 0.99 for Barcelona, the market is essentially calling it. But the 2025-26 season ends in May 2026, so a T-3d snapshot in late season would put us near May 2026.

Given the Kalshi market is highly liquid and at 0.99, I should respect it strongly. The DEEP_0 agent is an outlier based on early-season data and missed the snapshot's implication. The Halawi/AIA rule says not to be dragged by outliers.

iterations.content:
The search results show Barcelona's results into Feb 2026 are mixed (losses to Girona and Real Sociedad). The Kalshi price at 0.99 for Barcelona with T-3d suggests the question is resolving with Barcelona winning. Given the strong market signal at 0.99 (which is essentially the market saying "this is happening"), I should anchor close to it while leaving small probabilities for tail risks.

The four agents at 0.90-0.984 are aligned with the market. DEEP_0 is an outlier using early-season data and missed the T-3d snapshot signal. CALIBRATED_GPT5 at 0.61 is too conservative for a T-3d Kalshi @ 0.99.

args.reasoning: Key disagreement: 4 agents anchored to Kalshi T-3d snapshot (Barcelona @ 0.99) gave 0.90-0.984; DEEP_0 outlier gave Real Madrid 0.46 based on early 2025-26 season data (Real Madrid won El Clásico Oct 26 2025), and CALIBRATED_GPT5 gave 0.61 (too conservative given liquid market). DEEP_0 is a FACTUAL miss: it ignored the T-3d Kalshi snapshot which by definition reflects information at 3 days before resolution (late May 2026). My searches confirm the 2025-26 La Liga season is real and in progress, with mixed Barcelona form in early 2026 but the market clearly converged. A T-3d Kalshi snapshot at 0.990 with deep liquidity is an exceptionally strong signal — markets rarely sit at 0.99 with 3 days remaining unless mathematically near-clinched. Per AIA §5.2 / Halawi 2024, I should not be dragged toward the DEEP_0 outlier (Real Madrid 0.46) without strong evidence the market is wrong, and I have no such evidence. I anchor to the market at ~0.96 for Barcelona, leaving small residual mass for Real Madrid (0.025) and tiny tails for others, consistent with Kalshi pricing.
